text: The LockBit 3.0 ransomware group exploited CVE-2023-4966 in Citrix NetScaler ADC to gain initial access. After lateral movement, they deployed their payload across enterprise networks, stealing credentials and encrypting files on Windows Server and VMware ESXi hosts.

Security researchers identified a spear-phishing campaign targeting financial sector employees. Attackers sent convincing emails impersonating Microsoft 365 support, tricking users into entering credentials on a fake login page hosted on a compromised domain.

APT29, also known as Cozy Bear, leveraged a zero-day vulnerability CVE-2024-1234 in Microsoft Exchange Server to install the SUNBURST backdoor on government networks. The group used living-off-the-land techniques to avoid detection.

A distributed denial-of-service attack overwhelmed the infrastructure of a major European bank. The Mirai botnet, consisting of over 100,000 compromised IoT devices, generated traffic exceeding 1.2 Tbps, taking down online banking services for six hours.

Emotet malware was distributed through malicious Word documents attached to invoice-themed phishing emails. Once executed, the dropper downloaded TrickBot from command-and-control servers, enabling credential theft and follow-on ransomware deployment.

A supply chain attack compromised the build pipeline of 3CX Desktop App, injecting malicious code into version 18.12.407. The Lazarus Group used this backdoored software to target cryptocurrency exchanges and financial institutions.

CVE-2021-44228 (Log4Shell) was actively exploited in the wild to compromise Apache Log4j 2 instances. Attackers used JNDI injection to execute arbitrary code on vulnerable Java applications, leading to full system compromise on unpatched servers.

BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware operators used Cobalt Strike and legitimate remote management tools like AnyDesk to move laterally through a healthcare provider network. They exfiltrated 4TB of patient records before deploying the encryptor.

A watering hole attack compromised a popular industrial control system vendor's website, injecting malicious JavaScript that exploited CVE-2023-7890 in Internet Explorer 11 to deliver TRITON malware targeting Schneider Electric safety controllers.

The FIN7 threat group conducted a business email compromise scam targeting CFOs at manufacturing companies. By spoofing executive email addresses and exploiting misconfigured SPF records in Microsoft Office 365, they redirected wire transfers totaling $4.5M.

Clop ransomware exploited a zero-day SQL injection vulnerability CVE-2023-34362 in MOVEit Transfer to steal data from over 2,000 organizations without deploying encryption. The attack demonstrated a shift toward pure data extortion tactics.

A sophisticated DDoS attack using DNS amplification and NTP reflection techniques targeted a major DNS provider. The Killnet hacktivist group claimed responsibility for the 800 Gbps attack that disrupted internet services across multiple countries.

Researchers discovered that XZ Utils versions 5.6.0 and 5.6.1 contained a backdoor injected by threat actor JiaT75 over two years. The malicious code targeted OpenSSH on systemd-enabled Linux systems, enabling unauthorized remote code execution.

A phishing campaign attributed to TA453 (Charming Kitten) used fake conference invitations to deliver POWERSTAR malware to think tank researchers. The spear-phishing emails contained malicious Google Drive links that downloaded the backdoor.

Play ransomware group breached a regional hospital system by exploiting CVE-2022-41082 (ProxyNotShell) in Microsoft Exchange. They encrypted 120 servers and demanded a $4M ransom, disrupting patient care services for three weeks.

A large-scale data breach at a credit bureau exposed 147 million records including Social Security numbers, birth dates, and addresses. Attackers exploited an unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability CVE-2017-5638 to gain initial access to the web application.

MuddyWater APT deployed a new tool called PhonyC2 via spear-phishing PDFs targeting Middle Eastern government agencies. The framework used legitimate cloud services including OneDrive and Google Drive for command-and-control communications.

A botnet composed of 200,000 compromised routers launched a volumetric DDoS attack against a gaming company's servers. The attack peaked at 3.47 Tbps, surpassing the previous record, and combined UDP flood with ICMP fragmentation techniques.

A ransomware-as-a-service operation called RansomHub leveraged CVE-2024-3400 in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS to gain initial access, then used ZeroLogon CVE-2020-1472 for privilege escalation before encrypting Active Directory domain controllers.
